## Slice Plan Excerpt
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M003/slices/S02/S02-PLAN.md`
**Goal:** Страница /deals показывает колонки по stage order (все 8 стадий даже без сделок); drag-and-drop карточек между колонками вызывает API move и сохраняет relation-данные (stage, contact, manager) в UI; FilterBar фильтрует по статусу (open/closed); CreateDealModal создаёт сделку с возможностью привязки контакта
**Demo:** Страница /deals показывает колонки по stage order; drag-and-drop карточки между колонками вызывает API move; FilterBar фильтрует по статусу; CreateDealModal создаёт сделку

### Slice Verification
- Runtime signals: pipeline API returns standard JSON error shape { error, message } на 404/500; moveDeal errors log to console.error и trigger refetch
- Inspection surfaces: GET /api/pipelines и GET /api/pipelines/[id] доступны для curl/fetch диагностики; kanban board DOM содержит data-stage-id атрибуты на колонках
- Failure visibility: ошибка move возвращает 400/500 и текст ошибки, UI делает полный refetch deals; консольное логирование ошибок
- Redaction constraints: PII (contact names, manager names) отображается в UI но не содержится в логах ошибок



## UNIT: Execute Task T03 ("Add contact selector to CreateDealModal") — Slice S02 ("S02"), Milestone M003

# GSD context snapshot (2026-06-21T14:22:03.081Z)

## Top project memories
- [MEM019] (gotcha) Prisma 7.x breaks Prisma 6.x schema format: datasource `url` property moved to prisma.config.ts. Task specified Prisma 6.x, so locked apps/web to Prisma 6.6.0 to avoid breaking changes.
- [MEM009] (architecture) Prisma schema.prisma is single source of truth; SQLAlchemy uses automap_base() reflection, not declarative models. Python worker re-reflects schema on restart after Prisma migrations.
- [MEM014] (gotcha) S01 summary describes files (apps/web, docker-compose.yml, package.json) that don't actually exist in the repo. Current state has only apps/worker with FastAPI. Plan S06 CI/CD for actual code, not S01 summary claims. Web app CI/CD jobs will be added when apps/web is created later.
- [MEM018] (architecture) Using SQLite for development instead of PostgreSQL due to Docker Desktop unavailable. Schema datasource is sqlite (not postgresql). This is a temporary dev-only setup; production will use PostgreSQL.
- [MEM020] (architecture) Contact model is unified (type=person|company) per spec docs, not separate Company/Contact models. SQLite doesn't support String[] arrays — use Json with @default("[]") for tags field.
- [MEM022] (gotcha) Prisma schema for Contact requires manual id (no @default) and updatedAt (no @updatedAt auto-update). The ContactRepository must generate UUID via randomUUID() and set updatedAt on create. Other models like User have same pattern - check schema before assuming auto-generated fields.

## Handoff from plan-slice

**Intent:** Completed plan-slice for M003/S02

---

- No continue file present. Start from the top of the task plan.

- `.gsd/milestones/M003/slices/S02/tasks/T02-SUMMARY.md` — T02: Fixed moveDeal to return full relations, expanded KanbanColumn drop target to full column with drag-over highlight, and replaced alert() with server refetch on move errors | Fixed moveDeal to return full relations, expanded KanbanColumn drop target to full column with drag-over highlight, and replaced alert() with server refetch on move errors | decisions: Prisma include uses schema field names (DealStage/Pipeline/Contact/User), not the camelCase aliases used in API types (stage/pipeline/contact/manager) — field mapping happens at the API route boundary; Move relation fetching to the API route rather than the DealRepository to keep repository methods simple (single responsibility) and to contain Prisma naming conventions at the API boundary | key_files: apps/web/src/app/api/deals/[id]/move/route.ts; apps/web/src/components/deals/kanban-column.tsx; apps/web/src/app/deals/page.tsx

# T03: Add contact selector to CreateDealModal

Why: CreateDealModal creates deals with title/amount/currency/date but has no way to link a contact. For CRM purposes, most deals should be linked to a contact. The API accepts contactId (optional), so this is a gap in the UI.

Do:
1. Import contactsApi from @/lib/api/contacts. On modal open, fetch contacts via contactsApi.getContacts({}) — limited to first 50 results. Store in local state.
2. Add a search input (using the Input component) above or replacing a simple contact list. Filter contacts client-side by display name (case-insensitive substring match on name field). Debounce not strictly needed since filtering is client-side on already-fetched data.
3. Display filtered contacts in a scrollable list (max-h-40 overflow-y-auto) inside the modal. Each contact shows name and type badge. Click to select.
4. Add a "Контакт" Label section below the description field showing: a search input for filtering, a selected state (show contact name with an X button to clear), and the scrollable contact list (hidden when a contact is selected).
5. Add contactId to the DealCreateInput payload in handleSubmit. Make it optional.
6. Reset contact selection when modal closes or deal is created.

Done when: CreateDealModal has contact search; selecting a contact includes contactId in POST; deal created with contact shows contact name on kanban card; creating deal without contact still works.

## Inputs

- `apps/web/src/components/deals/create-deal-modal.tsx`
- `apps/web/src/lib/api/contacts.ts`
- `apps/web/src/lib/api/types.ts`

## Expected Output

- `apps/web/src/components/deals/create-deal-modal.tsx`

## Verification

cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit

7. Preserve useful observability for non-trivial async, API, background, or error-path work.

**Background process rule:** never run bare `command &`. Redirect output first, e.g. `command > /dev/null 2>&1 &`, or use `bg_shell` when available.

If verification fails, use one-hypothesis-at-a-time debugging: state the hypothesis, test it, change one variable, read complete functions/imports, separate facts from assumptions, and stop after 3 failed fixes to reset the model.

Keep about **~80K chars** for verification and summary. If context is nearly spent, stop implementation and write a resumable summary.

D:\CLAUDE\Project\unified-crm-finance\apps\web\src\app\api\contacts\route.ts

1→/**
     2→ * Contacts Collection API Endpoint
     3→ *
     4→ * CRUD API for Contact model:
     5→ * - GET: List all contacts (excluding soft-deleted)
     6→ * - POST: Create a new contact
     7→ *
     8→ * GET /api/contacts
     9→ * POST /api/contacts
    10→ */
    11→
    12→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
    13→import { contacts } from '../../../lib/db/contacts'
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * GET /api/contacts
    17→ *
    18→ * Returns all active contacts (excludes soft-deleted).
    19→ * Supports optional query parameters for filtering.
    20→ */
    21→export async function GET(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    22→  try {
    23→    const searchParams = request.nextUrl.searchParams
    24→    const type = searchParams.get('type')
    25→    const status = searchParams.get('status')
    26→
    27→    const where: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    28→    if (type) where.type = type
    29→    if (status) where.status = status
    30→
    31→    const allContacts = await contacts.findMany({
    32→      where: Object.keys(where).length > 0 ? where : undefined,
    33→      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    34→    })
    35→
    36→    return NextResponse.json({ data: allContacts, count: allContacts.length })
    37→  } catch (error) {
    38→    console.error('Failed to fetch contacts:', error)
    39→    return NextResponse.json(
    40→      { error: 'Failed to fetch contacts', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
    41→      { status: 500 }
    42→    )
    43→  }
    44→}
    45→
    46→/**
    47→ * POST /api/contacts
    48→ *
    49→ * Creates a new contact.
    50→ * Validates required fields based on contact type (person vs company).
    51→ */
    52→export async function POST(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    53→  try {
    54→    const body = await request.json()
    55→
    56→    // Validate contact type
    57→    if (!body.type || (body.type !== 'person' && body.type !== 'company')) {
    58→      return NextResponse.json(
    59→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'type must be either "person" or "company"' },
    60→        { status: 400 }
    61→      )
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Type-specific validation
    65→    if (body.type === 'person') {
    66→      if (!body.firstName) {
    67→        return NextResponse.json(
    68→          { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'firstName is required for person contacts' },
    69→          { status: 400 }
    70→        )
    71→      }
    72→    } else if (body.type === 'company') {
    73→      if (!body.companyName) {
    74→        return NextResponse.json(
    75→          { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'companyName is required for company contacts' },
    76→          { status: 400 }
    77→        )
    78→      }
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    // Phone is required for all contacts
    82→    if (!body.phone) {
    83→      return NextResponse.json(
    84→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'phone is required' },
    85→        { status: 400 }
    86→      )
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    // Prepare creation data (id/updatedAt filled by repository)
    90→    const createData = {
    91→      type: body.type,
    92→      phone: body.phone,
    93→      firstName: body.firstName || null,
    94→      lastName: body.lastName || null,
    95→      middleName: body.middleName || null,
    96→      companyName: body.companyName || null,
    97→      inn: body.inn || null,
    98→      kpp: body.kpp || null,
    99→      ogrn: body.ogrn || null,
   100→      email: body.email || null,
   101→      address: body.address || null,
   102→      physicalAddress: body.physicalAddress || null,
   103→      position: body.position || null,
   104→      notes: body.notes || null,
   105→      sourceId: body.sourceId || null,
   106→      ownerId: body.ownerId || null,
   107→      status: body.status || 'active',
   108→      tags: body.tags || [],
   109→      attributes: body.attributes || null,
   110→    }
   111→
   112→    const newContact = await contacts.create(createData)
   113→
   114→    return NextResponse.json({ data: newContact }, { status: 201 })
   115→  } catch (error) {
   116→    console.error('Failed to create contact:', error)
   117→    return NextResponse.json(

1→/**
     2→ * Deals Collection API Endpoint
     3→ *
     4→ * CRUD API for Deal model:
     5→ * - GET: List all deals (excluding soft-deleted) with optional filters
     6→ * - POST: Create a new deal
     7→ *
     8→ * GET /api/deals
     9→ * POST /api/deals
    10→ */
    11→
    12→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
    13→import { deals } from '../../../lib/db/deals'
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * GET /api/deals
    17→ *
    18→ * Returns all active deals (excludes soft-deleted).
    19→ * Supports optional query parameters for filtering.
    20→ */
    21→export async function GET(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    22→  try {
    23→    const searchParams = request.nextUrl.searchParams
    24→    const pipelineId = searchParams.get('pipelineId')
    25→    const stageId = searchParams.get('stageId')
    26→    const managerId = searchParams.get('managerId')
    27→    const contactId = searchParams.get('contactId')
    28→    const status = searchParams.get('status') // For filtering open/closed deals
    29→
    30→    const where: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    31→    if (pipelineId) where.pipelineId = pipelineId
    32→    if (stageId) where.stageId = stageId
    33→    if (managerId) where.managerId = managerId
    34→    if (contactId) where.contactId = contactId
    35→
    36→    // Filter by open/closed status
    37→    if (status === 'open') {
    38→      where.closedAt = null
    39→    } else if (status === 'closed') {
    40→      where.closedAt = { not: null }
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    const allDeals = await deals.findMany({
    44→      where: Object.keys(where).length > 0 ? where : undefined,
    45→      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    46→      include: {
    47→        stage: true,
    48→        pipeline: true,
    49→        contact: true,
    50→        manager: true,
    51→      },
    52→    })
    53→
    54→    return NextResponse.json({ data: allDeals, count: allDeals.length })
    55→  } catch (error) {
    56→    console.error('Failed to fetch deals:', error)
    57→    return NextResponse.json(
    58→      { error: 'Failed to fetch deals', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
    59→      { status: 500 }
    60→    )
    61→  }
    62→}
    63→
    64→/**
    65→ * POST /api/deals
    66→ *
    67→ * Creates a new deal.
    68→ * Validates required fields.
    69→ */
    70→export async function POST(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    71→  try {
    72→    const body = await request.json()
    73→
    74→    // Validate required fields
    75→    if (!body.title) {
    76→      return NextResponse.json(
    77→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'title is required' },
    78→        { status: 400 }
    79→      )
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    if (!body.pipelineId) {
    83→      return NextResponse.json(
    84→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'pipelineId is required' },
    85→        { status: 400 }
    86→      )
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    if (!body.stageId) {
    90→      return NextResponse.json(
    91→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'stageId is required' },
    92→        { status: 400 }
    93→      )
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    // Prepare creation data
    97→    const createData = {
    98→      title: body.title,
    99→      pipelineId: body.pipelineId,
   100→      stageId: body.stageId,
   101→      contactId: body.contactId || null,
   102→      amount: body.amount ?? 0,
   103→      currency: body.currency ?? 'RUB',
   104→      expectedCloseDate: body.expectedCloseDate || null,
   105→      managerId: body.managerId || null,
   106→      description: body.description || null,
   107→      lossReason: body.lossReason || null,
   108→      attributes: body.attributes || null,
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    const newDeal = await deals.create(createData)
   112→
   113→    return NextResponse.json({ data: newDeal }, { status: 201 })
   114→  } catch (error) {
   115→    console.error('Failed to create deal:', error)
   116→    return NextResponse.json(
   117→      { error: 'Failed to create deal', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
   118→      { status: 500 }
   119→    )
   120→  }
   121→}
   122→

arguments.oneLiner: Added contact search/selector to CreateDealModal with client-side filtering, type badges, and contactId in POST payload
arguments.narrative:
Modified CreateDealModal to fetch contacts via contactsApi.getContacts({}) on modal open (first 50 results). Added client-side filtering by display name with a search input, a scrollable contact list (max-h-40 overflow-y-auto), and type badges (Физ/Юр). Selected contact shows as a chip with an X button to clear. contactId is included in the DealCreateInput payload on submit. Contact selection resets on modal close or successful deal creation. No-contact creation still works (contactId stays undefined).

Display name is constructed from firstName+lastName for person contacts, or companyName for company contacts, with a fallback to contact.id. The API route already handles contactId gracefully (body.contactId || null).

No new TypeScript errors introduced. The contact-related imports, state, useEffect, handleSubmit contactId wiring, and JSX all type-check cleanly.

**Summary:** Modified `apps/web/src/components/deals/create-deal-modal.tsx` to add a contact selector:

- **Fetches contacts** via `contactsApi.getContacts({})` when modal opens (limited to 50)
- **Search/filter** — client-side substring filtering by constructed display name
- **Display name** — `firstName lastName` for persons, `companyName` for companies, fallback to `id`
- **Type badges** — `Физ` for person, `Юр` for company, using lucide `User`/`Building2` icons
- **Selection UI** — scrollable list (`max-h-40 overflow-y-auto`), click to select; selected contact shows as chip with X button to clear
- **contactId in POST** — `selectedContact?.id` is passed in the `DealCreateInput` payload (API already handles `contactId || null`)
- **Reset behavior** — contact selection clears on modal close or successful creation; creating deals without a contact continues to work

1→# M003: Сделки и контракты
     2→
     3→**Vision:** CRM модуль для управления сделками от лида до подписанного договора: kanban-доска с drag-and-drop по этапам, аудит перемещений через DealHistory, конвертация выигранной сделки в контракт, версионность контрактов, подписанты.
     4→
     5→## Success Criteria
     6→
     7→- Создание сделки через API с автонумерацией С-YYYY-NNNNN и привязкой к контакту
     8→- Drag-and-drop на kanban обновляет stage в БД и пишет DealHistory
     9→- Конвертация сделки создаёт Contract с bidirectional link (deal.contractId + contract.dealId)
    10→- Детальная страница сделки показывает DealHistoryTimeline с переходами между этапами
    11→- Детальная страница контракта показывает версии и подписантов
    12→- API routes протестированы, UI компоненты рендерятся без ошибок
    13→
    14→## Slices
    15→
    16→- [x] **S01: S01** `risk:low` `depends:[]`
    17→  > After this: curl POST /api/deals создаёт сделку с автонумерацией С-YYYY-NNNNN; PUT /api/deals/[id]/move меняет stage и пишет DealHistory; GET /api/deals возвращает сделки с фильтрами
    18→
    19→- [ ] **S02: S02** `risk:medium` `depends:[]`
    20→  > After this: Страница /deals показывает колонки по stage order; drag-and-drop карточки между колонками вызывает API move; FilterBar фильтрует по статусу; CreateDealModal создаёт сделку
    21→
    22→- [ ] **S03: Deal Detail Page and History Timeline** `risk:low` `depends:[S01]`
    23→  > After this: Страница /deals/[id] показывает карточку сделки с контактом, этапом, суммой; DealHistoryTimeline показывает историю переходов fromStage→toStage с датами и комментариями
    24→
    25→- [ ] **S04: Contract Repository, API, and Deal Conversion** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
    26→  > After this: POST /api/deals/[id]/convert создаёт Contract из Deal, устанавливает bidirectional link; ContractRepository.addVersion инкрементирует номер версии; ContractRepository.addSigner добавляет подписанта
    27→
    28→- [ ] **S05: Contract List and Detail Pages** `risk:low` `depends:[S04]`
    29→  > After this: Страница /contracts показывает список контрактов с поиском по контакту и фильтром по статусу; /contracts/[id] показывает tabs: Details, Versions, Signers, Related
